description: Shift from glottal fricative [h] to voiced velar stop [g] in loanword adaptation, often accompanied by uvular constriction.
sources: Kikniskis, T. (2008). _Georgian Phonotactics and Loanword Adaptation_. Tbilisi: Caucasus Press.
Harrison, D. (2015). _Uvular Consonants in Kartvelian Languages_. Journal of Caucasian Linguistics 12(3), 45-67.
